name: Backblaze B2
what_it_does: Low-cost S3-compatible object storage with industry-leading pricing, commonly used for backups, media storage, and cost-sensitive workloads.
use_cases: Cost-sensitive backup and archive storage for agent data
Large media file storage (video, images) with CDN via Cloudflare partnership
Development and testing storage without breaking the bank
not_for: Low-latency production workloads requiring sub-10ms response times
Teams needing comprehensive compliance certifications (limited vs AWS)
Complex workloads requiring advanced S3 features
best_when: Storage cost is a primary concern and you need reliable S3-compatible object storage for backups or archival.
avoid_when: You need low latency, full AWS S3 feature compatibility, or enterprise compliance certifications.

auth.notes: Application keys with configurable bucket and operation permissions. S3-compatible HMAC keys also supported. Straightforward auth for agents.

pricing.notes: Significantly cheaper than AWS S3 or GCS. Egress to Cloudflare network is free (partnership). 10GB free forever is a genuine free tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Both native B2 API and S3-compatible API. Native API error messages are functional but S3 compatibility layer errors can be confusing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Uploads are idempotent by filename. Large file multipart uploads have some caveats.
known_agent_gotchas: Two APIs (native B2 and S3-compatible) - pick one and stick with it
Download URLs change based on bucket type (public vs private)
File versioning is on by default and can accumulate storage costs if agents overwrite frequently
Limited data centers (only US and EU) vs AWS's global reach
